passages.title: Ideal Homes
passages.source_html:
Ideal Homes


New ideas and some old ones


A


The traditional kampung houses of Malaysia do not need air-conditioning. Built on stilts and with steep roofs, they have year-round ventilation. The raised structure ensures a cooling breeze comes up through the floorboards, while the high roof acts as a chimney to release hot air. The airtight, concrete boxes of modern city construction, in contrast, are heat traps, says Muhammad Peter Davis of University Putra Malaysia. He has calculated that typical modern Malaysian houses are 5℃ hotter than the air outside. The builders of kampung houses 'had no knowledge of modern science or engineering but they came up with the perfect design,' says Davis.


B


This story of ancient architectural sophistication and modern insanity is repeated around the world. In the name of modernism, people have thrown away architecture designed to cope with the environment in which they live, and adopted house designs originating from cold, northern environments, where the idea is to keep heat in.


C


Once, the aim of architectural modernists was to build structures that kept nature out and to replace it with air-conditioning. Now they are learning that they cannot do that effectively. Slowly, they are seeing the benefit of working with nature, rather than against it. In California, they probably use more power for air-conditioning than anywhere else on Earth. According to Arthur Rosenfeld from the University of California, what California needs is white paint. If Los Angeles painted its roofs white, planted trees to shade buildings, and chose lighter-colored paving, it could reduce city temperatures by 3℃ and cut the annual air-conditioning bill by $US 170 million a year.


D


Modern buildings are greedy in their use of energy. Much is made of the contribution of transportation to global warming, through its emissions of greenhouse gases. But, globally, transportation is responsible for just 22 per cent of carbon dioxide emissions. The building sector is responsible for 31 per cent, mostly the result of heating and air-conditioning systems. In developing countries, where demand for electricity for buildings is rising by 5 per cent a year, the biggest demand is for air-conditioning in modern buildings which are often designed to cope with every different climatic condition.


E


Before air-conditioning, much of the Arab world kept cool through thoughtful building design. Many buildings were built according to the principles of the wind tower, a tall structure with vertical vents at the top that open in all directions to catch any passing breeze. Typically, these wind-tower buildings were made of local materials such as stone, mud brick, wood and palm-tree fronds. The buildings were inward-looking, which served the dual function of focusing attention on the courtyard, where family members spend time together, and protecting living areas from the rays of the sun.


F


Throughout the Middle East today, wind towers are often little more than museum piece. But there are exceptions, Jordan has won awards for the architecture of a village on the shores of the Red Sea, which is designed to conserve energy. Made of rough, local stone in a traditional style, it combines the ventilation system of the wind towers for summer coolness with up-to-date heating and floor insulation to protect against the desert cold in winter.


G


Traditional building materials, like traditional building designs, are being rediscovered by those looking for low-energy solutions to the current construction needs. Clay is one such material: As the Dutch housing contractor R van der Ley has argued in promoting a series of clay housing projects in developing countries, clay has many benefits over its industrialized version, brick. Clay blocks cost only half as much as ordinary bricks. Clay also generates work because people can find it, mould it, bake it and work it themselves. Two hundred clay bricks can be made with the fuel oil that makes just one ordinary brick. Moreover, clay is an excellent insulator against both cold and heat outside, and can easily be recycled.


H


But although traditional methods and materials can be revived in appropriate settings, new green, low-energy technologies are needed, especially in urban environments. The thermal insulation of homes in cold countries is an example. In 1983, Sweden adopted a national standard, requiring the country’s homes to lose no more than 60 kilowatt-hours per square more over the year. To meet that standard, windows were double-glazed, and walls and roofs insulated. Every home became a fortress against the cold air outside. Unfortunately, the rest of Europe has not followed the lead.


I


In the United States, Amory Lovins has promoted a range of low-energy technologies: 'super-windows', for example, which let in invisible light but can be ‘tuned’ to either allow in, or reflect away, infrared solar radiation – the stuff that heats. Buildings with large expanses of window (and big energy bills) can be designed to achieve optimum temperatures.


J


As well as more efficient use of energy, the world also needs new sources of renewable power. Solar energy is being tried out in the developing world, in villages often far from electricity grids. The world's biggest solar power installation got underway in the Philippines, in 2001, where a project commenced to install solar panels for 400,000 people in 150 villages. Such projects are demonstrating that countries, whether developing or developed, no longer need huge national grids to supply electricity. Every home can do its own thing with the help of a solar panel and a storage battery.


K 


Houses, of course, are more than machines for living in. They are social and psychological spaces, too. Future houses may not look exactly like kampungs or wind towers or any other traditional models. But to work for us and for our environment, they will have to suit us as well as these traditional forms did.

groups.title: Questions 14-26
groups.source_html:
Questions 14 – 18


Look at the eleven paragraphs (A – K). Which paragraph contains the following information?


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
14

14 reasons why a particular construction material is advantageous
✅ 正确答案：G

审题：题目问的是哪个段落讲述了一种建筑材料特别有益的原因。


定位关键信息：“construction material”和“advantageous”


对应原文：G段，"Clay is one such material: As the Dutch housing contractor R van der Ley has argued in promoting a series of clay housing projects in developing countries, clay has many benefits over its industrialized version, brick, Clay blocks cost only half as much as ordinary bricks."


解题思路：文章中提到了粘土比其工业化版本砖有许多好处，粘土块的成本仅为普通砖的一半。
15

15 an example of a construction design which benefits domestic interaction
✅ 正确答案：E

审题：题目问的是哪个段落讲述了一种建筑设计有利于家庭互动的例子。


定位关键信息：“construction design”和“domestic interaction”


对应原文：E段，"The buildings were inward-looking, which served the dual function of focusing attention on the courtyard, where family members spend time together, and protecting living areas from the rays of the sun."



解题思路：文章中提到了风塔建筑的设计使得家庭成员可以在庭院里共度时光，这有利于家庭互动。
16

16 a description of a house that is ventilated from below
✅ 正确答案：A

审题：题目问的是哪个段落对一种从下面通风的房子的描述。


定位关键信息：“house”和“ventilated from below”


对应原文：A段，"The raised structure ensures a cooling breeze comes up through the floorboards, while the high roof acts as a chimney to release hot air. The airtight, concrete boxes of modern city construction, in contrast, are heat traps, says Muhammad Peter Davis of University Putra Malaysia."


解题思路：文章中提到了马来西亚传统的kampung房子是通过高架结构实现从底部通风的。
17

17 an example of self-sufficient energy supply
✅ 正确答案：J

审题：题目问的是哪个段落讲述了一个自给自足的能源供应的例子。


定位关键信息：“self-sufficient energy supply”


对应原文：J段，"Such projects are demonstrating that countries, whether developing or developed, no longer need huge national grids to supply electricity. Every home can do its own thing with the help of a solar panel and a storage battery."


解题思路：文章中提到了在菲律宾的一个项目，通过太阳能和储存电池，每个家庭都可以自给自足地供电。
18

18 suggested methods of reducing temperatures in a city
✅ 正确答案：C

审题：题目问的是哪个段落讲述了在城市中降低温度的建议方法。


定位关键信息：“methods”和“reducing temperatures”


对应原文：C段，"According to Arthur Rosenfeld from the University of California, what California needs is white paint. If Los Angeles painted its roofs white, planted trees to shade buildings, and chose lighter-colored paving, it could reduce city temperatures by 3℃ and cut the annual air-conditioning bill by SUS 170 million a year."


解题思路：文章中提到了通过使用白色油漆、种植树木以及选择浅色的铺路材料来降低城市温度的方法。

Questions 19 – 22


Choose the correct idea (A – G) for each person. 


List of ideas
A The choice of a certain construction material can have a socio-economic impact.
B 

Throughout the world, people are rejecting traditional housing design in order

to appear modern.

C 

Houses should not only meet people's physical needs but also their social and

psychological needs.

D Traditional knowledge can be superior to modern knowledge.
E There is an innovation that can save costs on both air-conditioning and heating.
F 

Solar energy can meet the energy needs of people living in villages in

developing countries.

G There is a very simple solution that can save on the cost of air-conditioning


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
19

19 Muhammad Peter Davis
✅ 正确答案：D

审题：题目问Muhammad Peter Davis的观点是什么。


定位关键信息：关键词为“Muhammad Peter Davis”。


对应原文：A段，“The builders of kampung houses 'had no knowledge of modern science or engineering but they came up with the perfect design,' says Davis”。


解题思路：原文提到Davis认为传统的马来西亚高架房屋设计没有使用现代科技，但其设计效果非常好，说明传统知识有时优于现代知识。
20

20 Arthur Rosenfeld
✅ 正确答案：G

审题：题目问的是Arthur Rosenfeld对应的观点。


定位关键信息：“Arthur Rosenfeld”


对应原文：C段，"According to Arthur Rosenfeld from the University of California, what California needs is white paint. If Los Angeles painted its roofs white, planted trees to shade buildings, and chose lighter-colored paving, it could reduce city temperatures by 3℃ and cut the annual air-conditioning bill by SUS 170 million a year.”


解题思路：Arthur Rosenfeld声称，如果洛杉矶将屋顶涂成白色，种植树木为建筑物遮阳，并选择浅色路面，它可以使城市温度降低3℃，每年减少1.7亿美元的空调费用。因此他认为一个非常简单的解决方案可以节省空调成本。
21

21 R van der Ley
✅ 正确答案：A

审题：题目问的是R van der Ley对应的观点。


定位关键信息：“R van der Ley”


对应原文：G段，"As the Dutch housing contractor R van der Ley has argued in promoting a series of clay housing projects in developing countries, clay has many benefits over its industrialized version, brick. Clay blocks cost only half as much as ordinary bricks.Clay also generates work because people can find it, mould it, bake it and work it themselves. "


解题思路：原文提到R van der Ley认为clay blocks有更多的价值，比如价格更加便宜，可以创造更多就业岗位，从而对社会经济产生影响。
22

22 Amory Lovins
✅ 正确答案：E

审题：题目问的是Amory Lovins对应的观点。


定位关键信息：“Amory Lovins”


对应原文：I段，"In the United States, Amory Lovins has promoted a range of low-energy technologies: 'super-windows', for example, which let in invisible light but can be ‘tuned’ to either allow in, or reflect away, infrared solar radiation – the stuff that heats."


解题思路： Amory Lovins推广了一种可以节省空调和供暖成本的创新技术——超级窗户（super-windows）。

Questions 23 – 26


Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text, choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, or choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

23

The air temperature in modern Malaysian houses is lower than the air temperature outside.

正确答案： B. FALSE

审题：题目问的是现代马来西亚房屋里的空气温度是否低于外面的空气温度。


定位关键信息：“modern Malaysian houses”和“air temperature”


对应原文：A段，"He has calculated that typical modern Malaysian houses are 5℃ hotter than the air outside."


解题思路：文章中提到现代马来西亚房屋比外部空气温度高5℃，因此答案为FALSE。

24

The construction industry is more to blame than transport for global warming.

正确答案： A. TRUE

审题：题目问的是建筑行业是否比交通对全球变暖负有更大的责任。


定位关键信息：“construction industry”"transport"和“global warming”


对应原文：D段，"But, globally, transportation is responsible for just 22 per cent of carbon dioxide emissions. The building sector is responsible for 31 per cent, mostly the result of heating and air-conditioning systems."


解题思路：文章中提到交通占二氧化碳排放量的22%，而建筑行业则占31%，因此建筑行业比交通对全球变暖负有更大的责任,答案为TRUE。

25

The use of wind towers for cooling is widespread in the Middle East today.

正确答案： B. FALSE

审题：题目问的是在中东地区使用风塔进行冷却是否普遍。


定位关键信息：“wind towers”和“Middle East”


对应原文：F段，"Throughout the Middle East today, wind towers are often little more than museum piece."


解题思路：文章中提到在中东地区今天风塔往往只是博物馆的展品，不是普遍现象，因此答案为FALSE。

26

The 'super-windows' promoted by Amory Lovins can be installed at low cost.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

审题：题目问的是Amory Lovins推广的“超级窗户”是否可以低成本安装。


定位关键信息：“Amory Lovins”和“super-windows”


对应原文：I段，"In the United States, Amory Lovins has promoted a range of low-energy technologies: 'super-windows', for example, which let in invisible light but can be ‘tuned’ to either allow in, or reflect away, infrared solar radiation – the stuff that heats. Buildings with large expanses of window (and big energy bills) can be designed to achieve optimum temperatures."


解题思路：文章中对于super- windows的描述没有提及是否可以低成本安装,因此答案为NOT GIVEN。
